channel.title: Untagged on Fernando Correia's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Untagged on Fernando Correia's Blog

item.title: Scratch
item.description:
Arithmetic

We can perform arithmetic in Go with literals (or named values, covered in the next exercise) using the following operators:

  • + to add
  • - to subtract
  • * to multiply
  • / to divide
  • % to take the remainder (the modulus operator) between two numbers.

Go has the following arithmetic operations that perform calculations and update the variable’s value:

+= to add to the variable. -= to subtract from the variable. *= to multiply the variable by a factor. /= to divide the variable by a dividend.
